author: madmanu01
content:
**Subject: `eth_*` state methods — block param required vs default-to-latest** (ref: [NethermindEth/nethermind#11764](https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/issues/11764))

Nethermind currently defaults the block parameter to `latest` when it's omitted on the 5 state-reading methods: `eth_getBalance`, `eth_getStorageAt`, `eth_getTransactionCount`, `eth_getCode`, `eth_getProof`.
The [execution-apis spec](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/blob/main/src/eth/state.yaml) marks the Block param `required: true` for all of these methods, so NM's default-to-latest behaviour is non-compliant and other clients may reject requests that omit the block param.
Internally we evaluate that defaulting to `latest` is ergonomic and consistent with how NM treats several other endpoints, so some of us see it as a feature rather than a bug.
**Proposal:** relax the spec, mark the Block param `required: false` (default `latest`) for the state methods, matching the already-optional behaviour of `eth_call` / `eth_estimateGas` / `eth_createAccessList` in [`execute.yaml`](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/blob/main/src/eth/execute.yaml). That would make the default-to-latest behaviour spec-legal and let other clients opt in.

author: madmanu01
content: Would like to discuss in the next RPC Standards call. Curious whether other teams would also prefer the relaxed default.

author: mysticryuujin
content: The problem with "opt-in" is that it allows developers to write shitty code that works with one client because they happen to be testing with that one client, that then doesn't work with the other 4+ clients and breaks as soon as they switch RPC providers. It's the same argument we had with Besu about accepting decimal numbers over "0x" hex strings.
